venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: Live traditional and contemporary bluegrass music.

Smoking Toad plays a funky blend of rock 'n' roll, blues and R&B, including both original tunes and covers of artists such as Lou Reed, James Brown, Jimi Hendrix, Buddy Miles, Lynyrd Skynyrd, and Taj Mahal.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Darin Savage plays Spanish guitar every Monday evening.
Amy Mann + expectoration + Americana/folk = Amy Coffman.
A night of pure death metal by bands with logos that are either intentionally designed to be totally illegible, or are rejects from the Magic Eye book series.

Join the Brotha Love & The Blueristocrats house band for a fun-filled night out in Santa Fe, as they are joined every week by some of the finest variety of traditional blues with a little honky-tonk, rock, and soul, by players from Albuquerque to Taos and beyond.

artist: Remix Wednesdays feat. DJ Stranger Vinyl Set
description: Enjoy "Night Snacks with a Soundtrack" at Remix Audio Bar with DJ Stranger curating an all vinyl hip hop set. Try our in house made ice cream for dessert.
An unforgettable evening of live country music, good vibes, and great company.

It's that time again-- Time to join the yaught rockin' John Francis and his merry bunch for the last Wednesday of the month at La Reina!  There will be songs; there will be mezcal; there will be a bunch of Santa Fe transplants in cowboy boots who are a little bit mean.
Share happy hour with Santa Fe's own musical legend Bill Hearne.
Maus plays blues, soul and pop on piano and vocals.
This summer, the heart of the SAR campus turns into an outdoor cocktail lounge and live music venue for Music at Dusk starting off with the original world folk music of Round Mountain.
Grab your instrument and join Queen Bee Music Association for a folk jam! All experience levels welcome!

artist: Folk Jam with Queen Bee Music Association
description: A folk jam open to all levels and all instruments.
Caleb & Company plays a wide variety of music spanning from Ray Charles to Radiohead. So no matter who you've been listening to lately, there will be something for you.
Talking Heads and David Byrne cover act Heartbyrne will be playing the Railyard as part of Lensic360's summerific concert series. Show up to this one and you will surely be up all night.
DJ Optamystik taking requests and mixing all your favorites from oldies to today's hits mixing up all genres.
The most positive beats in town every Thursday.
A Spanish bassist from Valencia, Vincen Garcia is a world renowned jazz and funk musician.  Now, he's bringing his groovy jams to the acoustically-experimental concert hall at Tumbleroot.
Experience classic country favorites and timeless western sounds with Desperados during Après Music at The Santa Fe Teahouse.

Blending western swing, country, and Americana influences, the band brings lively musicianship and timeless songs to one of Santa Fe's favorite gathering places. It's the perfect soundtrack for an afternoon spent relaxing on our year-round patio after exploring Canyon Road.
Bryan Hutchinson performs de Falla, Granados and Chopin on piano.
Two powerhouse bands that ignite the stage with Latin rock, Chicano funk, and timeless old-school classics.

venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: Supported by 'Narrow Head' and 'she's green', Virginia rockband turnover is playing the outdoor stage at The Bridge

venue: The Mystic Santa Fe, Cerrillos Road, Santa Fe, NM, USA
description: Madrid-based Benedeadly serves up psychedelic spaghetti western and freak folk with her full band, joined each month by a rotating cast of special guests and opening sets from visiting friends and kindred artists.
Deep cut cumbias y más with DJ Luz Skylarker.

Maus plays blues, soul and pop on piano and vocals.

Get ready to turn up the energy for PRIDE {after dark}, one of the most electrifying nights of PRIDE Santa Fe 2026 featuring an immersive, late-night dance experience where music, art, and queer joy collide.
Bring your dancing shoes for this award winning pop, rock and funk performance.
La Reina is bringing their signature cocktails outdoors alongside some pop-up pizza from Tender Fire as part of their Summer Sundays series!  Music starts at 5 thanks to the Carson Barry Trio.

description: Jazzy jazz and bluesy blues come together for some of that good ol' jump, jive and wailin'. Jam band fands? Take note.

Local multi-hyphenate Raashan Ahmad presides over his annual ode to hip-hop's genesis and enduring excellence with performances from Souls of Mischief, Brother Ali and Slum Village. Honestly? This lineup is INSANE and the show is free. Thanks, Raashan and Lensic 360!
This-here Denver band blends jazz, electro swing and pysch-rock into this almost bluegrassy-but-also-its-own-thing kind of thing. Part of the Summer Scene series.

venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: The show with Callahan (aka/fka Smog) proved so popular in the ticket sale window that Lensic 360 brass had to move it to a bigger venue. For the record, that venue is The Bridge and the style is DIY and lo-fi indie rock. It's honestly so so good what this dude does. Producer/performer Frances Chang opens the show.

The Oakland singer-songwriter returns for what is starting to feel almost like an annual Santa Fe appearance. This one's for fans of soul, R&B and roots and blues and Grammy winners who are also apparently super-nice dudes.
David Wood plays the American Songbook on grand piano every Friday evening.
Austinite Morales comes to the farm with her mix of Mexican- and American-inspired ranchera, rock, folk and Americana. Part of the free Summer Scene series.

venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: If you don't know who this band is by now, the show is probably not for you. As for everyone else living with Warren Haynes reverence? Your ship has come in. Please note there are multiple ticket tiers, parking options and more vie the Lensic 360 website (click that "website" button above).
Producer/performer Yung Bae pulls from his extensive catalog of dance jamz that feature the likes of funk, hip-hop and disco—plus influences like video games. anime and being a little weird.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.

venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: Pepper and The Movement are two different bands, btw, and not, as we thought, the name of one band. Cool? Cool. The former blends reggae and rock with punk-ish leanings, while the latter is all about alt.reggae. Word.

description: Live traditional and contemporary bluegrass music.
